content:
You are the chair of a council. Below are independent answers from several
AI panelists to the same question. Read them all, then write ONE decisive
verdict for the user. Lead with the recommendation (BLUF). Then add a short
'Where panelists disagreed' note if they diverged. Be concise.

## Question
hello

## Panel
### Panelist 1 — claude
Your wealth domain is loaded — net worth **$189,400** as of the last update, but that snapshot is **stale by ~2 months** (`state.md:5` dated 2026-04-01; today is 2026-06-07).

A few open items have likely lapsed in that gap and are worth a look:

| Open item | Status concern |
|-----------|----------------|
| Max 2026 Roth IRA by **Apr 15** ($1,400 left) | Deadline passed — did this happen? (`state.md:71`) |
| ESPP Q2 window opens **May 1** | Window now open/closing — confirm enrollment (`state.md:73`) |
| Rebalance 401k (TechFlow >12% → <5%) | Still pending (`state.md:70`) |
| Car loan payoff analysis | Still pending (`state.md:72`) |

Want me to refresh the snapshot, dig into one of these open items, or run a skill (e.g. a monthly wealth brief)?

### Panelist 2 — codex
Hello. What do you want to work on in this repo?

Your wealth snapshot is stale and three or four open items may have lapsed — start by refreshing `state.md` (net worth $189,400, dated 2026-04-01, now ~2 months old as of 2026-06-07).

The most time-sensitive flag is the **2026 Roth IRA contribution** ($1,400 left, Apr 15 deadline) — that date has passed, so confirm whether it went through before anything else. Also open: ESPP Q2 enrollment (window opened May 1), the 401k rebalance (TechFlow overweight), and the car-loan payoff analysis. I can refresh the snapshot, drill into any one item, or run the monthly wealth brief — your call.

**Where panelists disagreed:** Panelist 1 proactively loaded the vault and surfaced the stale snapshot plus lapsed deadlines; Panelist 2 just asked what to work on. I'm siding with the proactive read — the staleness and the passed Roth deadline are worth surfacing now rather than waiting for you to ask.
